_comment: This is an anonymised example UCS profile. All data is fictional and for illustration purposes only.

domains.name: product strategy
domains.notes: Particularly strong in early-stage positioning and market narrative

domains.name: AI infrastructure
domains.notes: Growing fast — focused on middleware, memory systems, and identity layers

domains.name: open source governance
domains.notes: RFC processes, foundation models, contributor management

domains.name: venture fundraising
domains.notes: Seed and Series A dynamics, VC signalling, deck structure

domains.name: regulatory strategy
domains.notes: EU AI Act, GDPR portability provisions, AI portability policy landscape

active.name: Universal Cognitive Schema
active.description: Open standard for portable AI identity across platforms. Building the neutral middleware layer that enables cognitive state migration between AI models.
active.context_summary: UCS is an open schema standard (MIT licensed) defining 7 dimensions of AI identity: persona, expertise map, project graph, preference corpus, interaction patterns, trust boundaries, and temporal context. The project is at v0.1.0-alpha. GitHub repo is live. Next steps: sample profile, CONTRIBUTING.md, SECURITY.md, first HN post. Long-term goal: IETF Internet-Draft submission, independent foundation governance.

active.name: UCS SaaS platform
active.description: Commercial migration and identity persistence product built on top of the UCS standard.
active.context_summary: Consumer-facing product: extract cognitive profile from any major AI platform, normalise to UCS, inject into destination. V1 scope: ChatGPT → Claude migration only. Revenue model: free migration, paid sync subscription, enterprise API. Target raise: $3-8M seed. Key differentiator from Mem0: consumer migration UX vs developer memory infrastructure.

archived.name: AI platform research
archived.description: Research phase: mapping memory features, export formats, and lock-in mechanisms across ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Grok, Perplexity.
archived.context_summary: Completed. Key finding: no platform shares a common memory schema. Each defines memory differently. No standard possible from within the platforms. External neutral layer required.

custom.preferred_code_style: clean with inline comments on non-obvious logic

interaction_patterns.typical_prompt_style: Short, context-rich instructions. Assumes shared background. Often builds on previous turns without re-establishing context. Frequently uses hypotheticals and scenario framing.
common_corrections: response too long — cut to the point
too generic — make it specific to the situation
reinforced_behaviours: naming the real risk not the diplomatic version
showing reasoning steps for non-obvious conclusions
flagging when a question has a better framing
rejected_behaviours: repeating conclusions already established in the conversation
suggesting obvious next steps without being asked

sensitive_domains: external communications sent on behalf of user

persona.note: High confidence — inferred from consistent patterns across 2,400+ interactions

expertise_map.note: Good — 5 domains identified with clear depth differentiation

project_graph.note: Very high — projects explicitly structured and well-documented

preference_corpus.note: High — strong signal from repeated format corrections

interaction_patterns.note: Good — consistent patterns detected across topics

trust_boundaries.note: Moderate — inferred from autonomy-related corrections, not explicit statements

temporal_context.note: Good — life chapters explicitly defined, recency weight estimated
